Plaintiffs Jennifer Walker, And Andrea Walker'S Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs' Special Interrogatories (Set Three); And Form Interrogatories (Set Three And Four); And Request For Sanctions.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Morris Nazarian, Tel. No. 1-310-284-7333 appearing for the Plaintiff by Telephone appearance;
Atty. David W. Martin, Tel. No. 1-510-332-3943 appearing for the defendant Bao Nguyen.

RULING - The court confirmed its tentative ruling with the following proviso: The Court does not rule that res judicata bars any effort to assert alter ego liability against Mr. Nguyen as to secondhand job in a future post judgment motion. However, the complaint fails to allege such liability against Mr. Nguyen. Defendant Bao Nguyen's demurrer to all seven causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Jennifer and Andrea Walker is sustained without leave to amend as to all seven causes of action. While the judgment in the prior case in favor of Mr. Nguyen was on an alter ego claim not a fraudulent transfer claim and the facts alleged by the Walkers in this case support fraudulent transfer claims against Mr. Nguyen as "a person for whose benefit the transfer was made" (Qwest Communications Corp. v. Weisz (SD CA 2003) 278 F.Supp.2d 1188), all such claims could have been brought in the prior case and are thus barred by res judicata. "The doctrine [of res judicata] not only precludes re-litigation of claims resolved in a prior action, but it also precludes litigation of claims that could have been brought in the prior action but were not." (Franceschi v. Franchise Tax Board (2016) 1 Cal. App. 5th 247, 258). The Walkers' trial brief in the prior case makes it clear that all facts supporting their claims in this case were known to the Walkers at the time of the trial in the prior case and were sought to be used to support the imposition of alter ego liability against Mr. Nguyen in the prior case. As but one of many references in that trial brief to the same facts alleged in this case, the Walkers' trial brief stated at page 10 of that brief that Mr. Nguyen "transferred Hand Job to his brother in July 2015'that Hand Job continued to conduct business'After he transferred the business to his brother, Defendant Nguyen closed the only bank account that Hand Job maintained causing Hand Job to become insolvent." Because the Walkers could have brought all of their fraudulent transfer claims against Mr. Nguyen in the prior case, all those claims are now precluded by res judicata. Parties to meet and confer as to form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-08-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Craig A. Brandt, Esq., T:510.601.1309, for defendants/opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER WALKER and ANDREA WALKER'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT, as to all 29 affirmative defenses in the answer to the complaint filed by defendants Kevin Tuan, Kristie Phung and Hand Job, LLC is a) SUSTAINED with ten days leave to amend as to the first through fourth, seventh, eighth, seventeenth, and twenty-first affirmative defenses, b) OVERRULED as to the fifth, sixteenth, eighteenth, nineteenth, twenty-second through twenty-fourth and twenty-eighth affirmative defenses, and c) SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the sixth, ninth through fifteenth, twentieth, twenty-fifth through twenty-seventh and twenty-ninth affirmative defenses. Defendants' request for leave to amend certain of their affirmative defenses is granted and comports with California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings. Defendants' withdrawal of some of their affirmative defenses requires that the Walkers' demurrer to those affirmative defenses be sustained without leave to amend. As to the remainder of the affirmative defenses, the demurrer serves no useful purpose and unnecessarily increases the cost of litigating this case. If the Walkers believe that they truly need more information about those defenses, they should propound discovery seeking contention information about those defenses. The Walkers are admonished not to file a demurrer to the amended answer.

Counsel for the defendants is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.
